---
title: "Lecture 12 Quiz: JavaScript Variables & Data Types"
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

::: {.callout-note}
Review before quiz:
[Lectures](/lectures/lecture-12.qmd) | [Week 3 Content](/content/week-03.qmd) | [Lab 3](/labs/lab-03.qmd) | [Assignment 3](/assignment/03-assignment.qmd)
:::

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
using QuizQuestions
```

## Lecture 12 Self-Check

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `var`", "B. `let`", "C. `const`", "D. `int`"]
question = "Question 1: Which keyword should be preferred for values that should not be reassigned?"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. Number", "B. Boolean", "C. String", "D. Object"]
question = "Question 2: Which JavaScript type represents textual data?"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `==`", "B. `===`", "C. `=`", "D. `!=`"]
question = "Question 3: Which operator checks strict equality (value and type)?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `push()`", "B. `trim()`", "C. `split()`", "D. `charAt()`"]
question = "Question 4: Which method adds an element to the end of an array?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `let name = 'A'`", "B. `const my-name = 'A'`", "C. `const 2name = 'A'`", "D. `let if = 'A'`"]
question = "Question 5: Which declaration follows JavaScript variable naming rules?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```
